content.problemStatement:
Chef visited a grocery store for fresh supplies. There are NNN items in the store where the ithi^{th}ith item has a freshness value AiA_iAi​ and cost BiB_iBi​.

Chef has decided to purchase **all** the items having a freshness value **greater than equal to** XXX. Find the total cost of the groceries Chef buys.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the number of items and the minimum freshness value an item should have.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers, the array AAA, denoting the freshness value of each item.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers, the array BBB, denoting the cost of each item.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the total cost of the groceries Chef buys.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains two integers N and X — the number of items and the minimum freshness value. The second line contains N integers A_i, the freshness values. The third line contains N integers B_i, the costs of the items.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total cost of all items whose freshness value is greater than or equal to X.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Only the second item has freshness >=20, cost 90.
Test case 2: All three items have freshness >=1, total cost 1+2+3=6.
Test case 3: No item reaches freshness 100, total cost 0.
Test case 4: Items 2 and 3 meet freshness >=50, costs 30+20=50.
The single item has freshness 5 which equals X, so Chef buys it. Total cost = 100.
No item has freshness >=10, therefore Chef buys nothing and the total cost is 0.

solution.approach: For each test case we iterate over the N items once. While iterating we check if A_i >= X; if true we add B_i to a running sum. After processing all items we output the sum. This is optimal because every item must be examined at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T * N) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it); X = next(it)
        A = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        B = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        total = sum(b for a, b in zip(A, B) if a >= X)
        out_lines.append(str(total))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
